title: woof EPIC.md front-matter
description: YAML front-matter of .woof/epics/E<N>/EPIC.md. Captures Definition-stage commitments: observable outcomes (user-facing acceptance) and contract decisions (technical contracts realising those outcomes). Free-form prose may follow the front-matter; only the structured header is validated. After Definition closes, outcome IDs and contract-decision IDs are append-only; ID removal requires gate-driven deprecation. Contract decisions reference standard contract artefacts (OpenAPI documents, Pydantic models, JSON Schema files); woof verifies via the artefact's native tooling (schemathesis, Pydantic, ajv-cli) rather than reinventing validation.

epic_id.description: GitHub issue number. Same value as plan.json.epic_id.

title.description: Short epic title; mirrors gh issue title.

acceptance_criteria.description: Prose statements that, taken together, define epic completion.

open_questions.description: Discovery-stage questions that survived into Definition unresolved. Should be empty when Definition closes; non-empty signals a Stage 2 incompletion.

deprecated.description: True iff this outcome was deprecated post-Definition via gate revision. Deprecated IDs remain in the array for traceability; new work targets a replacement ID.

openapi_ref.description: JSON-Pointer-style reference into a project OpenAPI document. Example: 'spec/openapi.yaml#/paths/~1api~1v1~1comments/post'. Stage 5 Check 4 verifies conformance via schemathesis or equivalent.

pydantic_ref.description: Module-and-class reference to a Pydantic model. Example: 'webapp/schemas/comment.py:CommentCreate'. Stage 5 Check 4 imports the module and verifies the model class exists.

json_schema_ref.description: Path to a JSON Schema file. Example: 'schemas/audit-event.schema.json'. Stage 5 Check 4 validates the schema is shipped at the declared path.

notes.description: Free-form prose. Use for rationale, not for contract content (which lives in the referenced artefact).
